topic: Abbreviations, Acronyms & Full Forms — Complete SSC Reference

questions.text: What does NITI in 'NITI Aayog' stand for?
options: National Institute for Technology and Innovation
questions.explanation: NITI stands for 'National Institution for Transforming India.' The full name is NITI Aayog (National Institution for Transforming India Aayog). It replaced the Planning Commission in January 2015. The PM chairs NITI Aayog.

questions.text: SEBI was established as a statutory body in which year?
questions.explanation: SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India) was initially established as a non-statutory body in 1988. It became a statutory body (with legal powers) in 1992 through the SEBI Act, 1992. Its headquarters are in Mumbai, Maharashtra.

options: Micro Units Development and Refinance Agency
Micro Urban Development for Rural Areas
Ministry of Urban Development and Rural Administration
Micro Units Development and Rural Administration
questions.explanation: MUDRA = Micro Units Development and Refinance Agency. It was set up under the PM Mudra Yojana (PMMY) in 2015 to provide micro-enterprise loans in three tiers: Shishu (up to ₹50k), Kishor (₹50k–₹5L), and Tarun (₹5L–₹10L).

questions.text: What is the full form of GST?
questions.explanation: GST = Goods and Services Tax. It is a comprehensive indirect tax that replaced multiple central and state taxes. GST was implemented on July 1, 2017. It follows the 'One Nation, One Tax' principle with four rates: 5%, 12%, 18%, and 28%.

questions.text: IRDAI, the insurance regulator of India, is headquartered in which city?
questions.explanation: IRDAI (Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India) is headquartered in Hyderabad, Telangana. It was initially set up in Delhi in 1999 and moved to Hyderabad in 2001. This is frequently confused with SEBI (Mumbai) or RBI (Mumbai).

UNICEF = United Nations Children's Fund (originally United Nations International Children's Emergency Fund, 1946). The 'Emergency' and 'International' were dropped but the acronym was retained. HQ is in New York.

questions.text: What does LPG stand for in the context of India's 1991 economic reforms?
questions.explanation: In the economic context, LPG refers to Liberalisation, Privatisation, and Globalisation — the three pillars of the 1991 economic reforms initiated by Finance Minister Manmohan Singh under PM P. V. Narasimha Rao. This opened India's economy to foreign investment.

questions.text: NABARD is headquartered in which city?
questions.explanation: NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development) is headquartered in Mumbai. It was established in 1982 on the recommendations of the Shivaraman Committee. NABARD provides credit for agricultural and rural development in India.

questions.text: What does SWAYAM stand for (India's online education platform)?
options: Study Without Any Years of Academic Mentoring
Study Webs of Active Learning for Young Aspiring Minds
Self-paced Web Access for Youth Academic Modules
Students with Advanced Yearning for Modern Learning
questions.explanation: SWAYAM = Study Webs of Active Learning for Young Aspiring Minds. It is a government-run MOOC (Massive Open Online Course) platform launched in 2016, offering free online courses from class 9 to post-graduation.

questions.text: What does the 'B' in IBRD (the main World Bank arm) stand for?
questions.explanation: IBRD = International Bank for Reconstruction and Development — this is the main arm of the World Bank Group. 'Bank' is the B. The World Bank Group also includes IFC (International Finance Corporation), IDA, ICSID, and MIGA.

questions.text: NPA in banking context stands for:
questions.explanation: NPA = Non-Performing Asset. A loan or advance is classified as an NPA if the interest or principal amount has not been paid for more than 90 days. High NPA levels indicate stress in the banking system. It is also called a 'bad loan.'

options: International Council of Aeronautics and Operations
questions.explanation: ICAO = International Civil Aviation Organisation. It is a UN specialised agency headquartered in Montreal, Canada. It sets global standards for civil aviation safety, security, efficiency, and environmental protection.

questions.text: What does the 'D' in DRDO stand for?
questions.explanation: DRDO = Defence Research and Development Organisation. It is India's premier defence research body under the Ministry of Defence. Established in 1958, DRDO is headquartered in New Delhi and has over 50 laboratories across India.

questions.text: IRNSS, India's satellite navigation system, is also known as:
questions.explanation: IRNSS = Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System, also called NavIC (Navigation with Indian Constellation). It is India's own GPS with a 7-satellite constellation providing accuracy within 5 metres in India and up to 1,500 km around its borders.

questions.text: CRR (Cash Reserve Ratio) refers to the proportion of deposits that banks must keep:
questions.explanation: CRR = Cash Reserve Ratio is the minimum percentage of deposits that commercial banks must maintain as cash with the Reserve Bank of India. It is a monetary policy tool — RBI increases CRR to reduce money supply. SLR = deposits in liquid assets (different from CRR).

UGC = University Grants Commission. It is a statutory body (established 1956) under the Ministry of Education that coordinates higher education and provides recognition and grants to universities in India. It is headquartered in New Delhi.
